verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane protein localized to the Golgi apparatus as an enzyme in the glycosylation biosynthetic pathway. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is a Golgi-resident glycosyltransferase with an N-terminal signal sequence that directs it to the Golgi and functions as a transmembrane anchor within the Golgi. Type II membrane proteins with Golgi localization are endomembrane-resident, not surface-accessible. No therapeutic programs target B4GALT1 on the cell surface, no surface isoforms are documented, and the protein has no documented surface-trafficking or PM-targeting mechanism. The protein body does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane.
